volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 1437 - Section 1583)

sections.label: Section 1437
sections.original: μέθεσθε δʼ ἤδη χαίρετόν τʼ · οὐ γάρ μʼ ἔτι
sections.translation: Now release me , and farewell ; for nevermore will you behold me living .

sections.label: Section 1438a
sections.original: ὦ τάλαινʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Ah , wretched that I am !

sections.label: Section 1439
sections.original: μή τοί μʼ ὀδύρου .
sections.translation: Do not mourn for me .

sections.label: Section 1439a
sections.original: καὶ τίς ἄν σʼ ὁρμώμενον
sections.translation: And who would not mourn you , brother , when you are hurrying off

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: εἰς προὖπτον Ἅιδην οὐ καταστένοι , κάσι ;
sections.translation: to a death foreseen ?

sections.label: Section 1441
sections.original: εἰ χρή , θανοῦμαι .
sections.translation: If it is fated , then I must die .

sections.label: Section 1441a
sections.original: μὴ σύ γʼ , ἀλλʼ ἐμοὶ πιθοῦ .
sections.translation: No , no , listen to my prayer !

sections.label: Section 1442
sections.original: μὴ πεῖθʼ ἃ μὴ δεῖ .
sections.translation: Do not plead for what must not be .

sections.label: Section 1442a
sections.original: δυστάλαινά τἄρʼ ἐγώ , εἴ σου στερηθῶ .
sections.translation: Then I , indeed , am utterly wretched , if I must lose you !

sections.label: Section 1443a
sections.original: καὶ τῇδε φῦναι χἀτέρᾳ . σφὼ δʼ οὖν ἐγὼ
sections.translation: It rests with the divinity , this way or that . But as for you two ,

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: θεοῖς ἀρῶμαι μή ποτʼ ἀντῆσαι κακῶν ·
sections.translation: I pray to the gods that you may never meet with evil ; for in all men ’ s eyes you do not deserve to suffer . Polyneices exits .

sections.label: Section 1.1448
sections.original: κακὰ βαρύποτμὰ παρʼ ἀλαοῦ ξένου ,
sections.translation: Behold , new ills of heavy fate have newly come from the blind stranger ,

sections.label: Section 1.1450
sections.original: εἴ τι μοῖρα μὴ κιγχάνει .
sections.translation: unless , perhaps , fate is finding its goal . I cannot say that a purpose of the divinities is ever vain . Time sees all things forever , and raises up some things ,

sections.label: Section 1.1455
sections.original: τὰ δὲ παρʼ ἦμαρ αὖθις αὔξων ἄνω .
sections.translation: then on the next day raises others back up again . The sky resounds ! Zeus !

sections.label: Section 1.1457
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα τέκνα , πῶς ἄν , εἴ τις ἔντοπος ,
sections.translation: Children , children ! If there is any man still here , send him forth to bring back Theseus , excellent in all respects .

sections.label: Section 1.1459
sections.original: πάτερ , τί δʼ ἐστὶ τἀξίωμʼ ἐφʼ ᾧ καλεῖς ;
sections.translation: And what , father , is the purpose of your summons ?

sections.label: Section 1.1460
sections.original: Διὸς πτερωτὸς ἥδε μʼ αὐτίκʼ ἄξεται
sections.translation: This winged thunder of Zeus will soon lead me to Hades . So send someone with speed .

sections.label: Section 1.1463
sections.original: κτύπος ἄφατος διόβολος · ἐς δʼ ἄκραν
sections.translation: Listen ! With a louder noise this one crashes down unspeakably ,

sections.label: Section 1.1465
sections.original: δεῖμʼ ὑπῆλθε κρατὸς φόβαν .
sections.translation: hurled by Zeus ! The hair of my head stands up for fear , my soul is dismayed ; for again the lightning flashes in the sky . What end will it release ?

sections.label: Section 1.1470
sections.original: ἀφορμᾷ ποτʼ , οὐκ ἄνευ ξυμφορᾶς .
sections.translation: I fear it , for never does it fly forth in vain , or without serious results . O great Sky ! O Zeus !

sections.label: Section 1.1472
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες , ἥκει τῷδʼ ἐπʼ ἀνδρὶ θέσφατος
sections.translation: Children , the appointed end of life has reached this man ; he can turn from it no more .

sections.label: Section 1.1474
sections.original: πῶς οἶσθα ; τῷ δὲ τοῦτο συμβαλὼν ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: How do you know ? By what means do you understand this ?

sections.label: Section 1.1475
sections.original: καλῶς κάτοιδʼ · ἀλλʼ ὡς τάχιστά μοι μολὼν
sections.translation: I know it well . But let some one go , I pray you , as quickly as he can , and bring back the lord of this land .

sections.label: Section 2.1477
sections.original: ἔα ἔα , ἰδοὺ μάλʼ αὖθις ἀμφίσταται διαπρύσιος ὄτοβος .
sections.translation: Look ! Look ! Once again the piercing din is around us !

sections.label: Section 2.1480
sections.original: ἵλαος , ὦ δαίμων , ἵλαος εἴ τι γᾷ
sections.translation: Be merciful , divinity , be merciful , if you are bringing anything of gloom for the land which is our mother ! May I find you well disposed , and may I not , because I have cared for a man accursed , somehow obtain a favor without profit !

sections.label: Section 2.1486
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἐγγὺς ἁνήρ ; ἆρʼ ἔτʼ ἐμψύχου , τέκνα ,
sections.translation: Is the man near ? Will he find me still alive , children , and master of my senses ?

sections.label: Section 2.1488
sections.original: τί δʼ ἂν θέλοις τὸ πιστὸν ἐμφῦναι φρενί ;
sections.translation: And what is the pledge that you would like to have firm in your mind ?

sections.label: Section 2.1489
sections.original: ἀνθʼ ὧν ἔπασχον εὖ , τελεσφόρον χάριν
sections.translation: In return for his benefits ,

sections.label: Section 2.1490
sections.original: δοῦναί σφιν , ἥνπερ τυγχάνων ὑπεσχόμην .
sections.translation: I would grant him the fulfillment of the favor that I promised .

sections.label: Section 2.1491
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰὼ παῖ , βᾶθι βᾶθʼ , εἴτʼ ἄκρα ,
sections.translation: Hurry , my son , come to us ! If you chance to be in the glade sacrificing an ox to the sea-god Poseidon ,

sections.label: Section 2.1495
sections.original: δικαίαν χάριν παρασχεῖν παθών .
sections.translation: then come ! For the stranger thinks you worthy , you and your city and your friends , to receive just return for benefits . Hasten quickly , lord !

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.original: τίς αὖ παρʼ ὑμῶν κοινὸς ἠχεῖται κτύπος ,
sections.translation: What din is this that once more rings forth from you all , from my people as clearly as from the stranger ? Can a thunderbolt from Zeus be the cause , or rushing hail in its fierce onset ? When the god sends such a storm , forebodings of every sort may find a place .

sections.label: Section 1505
sections.original: ἄναξ , ποθοῦντι προυφάνης , καί σοι θεῶν
sections.translation: Lord , you have appeared at my desire , and to you some god granted noble fortune at this coming .

sections.label: Section 1507
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστίν , ὦ παῖ Λαΐου , νέορτον αὖ ;
sections.translation: And what new thing has now occurred , son of Laius ?

sections.label: Section 1508
sections.original: ῥοπὴ βίου μοι · καί σʼ ἅπερ ξυνῄνεσα
sections.translation: My life hangs in the balance ; and I wish to die without cheating you and this city of the promises I made .

sections.label: Section 1510
sections.original: τῷ δʼ ἐκπέπεισαι τοῦ μόρου τεκμηρίῳ ;
sections.translation: And what is the proof of your fate that you depend on ?

sections.label: Section 1511
sections.original: αὐτοὶ θεοὶ κήρυκες ἀγγέλλουσί μοι ,
sections.translation: The gods themselves herald the news to me , nor do they cheat me of any of the appointed signs .

sections.label: Section 1513
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας , ὦ γεραιέ , δηλοῦσθαι τάδε ;
sections.translation: What makes these things clear ? Tell me , old man .

sections.label: Section 1514
sections.original: αἱ πολλὰ βρονταὶ διατελεῖς τὰ πολλά τε
sections.translation: The thunder , crash after crash ; the lightning , flash after flash ,

sections.label: Section 1515
sections.original: στράψαντα χειρὸς τῆς ἀνικήτου βέλη .
sections.translation: hurled from the unconquered hand .

sections.label: Section 1516
sections.original: πείθεις με · πολλὰ γάρ σε θεσπίζονθʼ ὁρῶ
sections.translation: I am persuaded , for in much I find you a prophet whose voice is not false . Then say what must be done .

sections.label: Section 1518
sections.original: ἐγὼ διδάξω , τέκνον Αἰγέως , ἅ σοι
sections.translation: I will expound myself , son of Aegeus , the treasures which will be laid up for this city , such as age can never hurt .

sections.label: Section 1520
sections.original: χῶρον μὲν αὐτὸς αὐτίκʼ ἐξηγήσομαι ,
sections.translation: Immediately , with no hand to guide me , I will lead to the place where I must die . But as to that place , never reveal it to another man , neither where it is hidden , nor in what region it lies , so that it may be an eternal defence for you , better than many shields , better than the spear of neighbors which brings relief .

sections.label: Section 1525
sections.original: δορός τʼ ἐπακτοῦ γειτονῶν ἀεὶ τιθῇ .
sections.translation: But as for mysteries which speech may not profane , you will learn them yourself when you come to that place alone , since I cannot declare them either to any of these people , or even to my own children , though I love them .

sections.label: Section 1530
sections.original: ἀλλʼ αὐτὸς αἰεὶ σῷζε , χὤταν εἰς τέλος
sections.translation:
Reserve them always to yourself , and when you reach the end of life , reveal them to your eldest son alone , and let him reveal them to his successor in turn forever .
In this way you will keep this city unscathed by the men born of the Dragon ’ s teeth . Countless cities commit outrage

sections.label: Section 1535
sections.original: κἂν εὖ τις οἰκῇ , ῥᾳδίως καθύβρισαν .
sections.translation: even though their neighbor commits no sin . For the gods are slow to punish , yet they are sure , when men scorn holiness and turn to frenzy . Do not desire this , son of Aegeus ! But you know such things as these without my teaching .

sections.label: Section 1540
sections.original: χῶρον δʼ , ἐπείγει γάρ με τοὐκ θεοῦ παρόν ,
sections.translation: Let us now set forth to that place — the divine summons urges me — and hesitate no longer .

sections.label: Section 1542
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες , ὧδʼ ἕπεσθʼ · ἐγὼ γὰρ ἡγεμὼν
sections.translation: Children , follow me . For now in turn it is I that shine forth wondrously as a leader for you , as you were your father ’ s . Onward . Do not touch me , but

sections.label: Section 1545
sections.original: αὐτὸν τὸν ἱερὸν τύμβον ἐξευρεῖν , ἵνα
sections.translation:
allow me unaided to find the sacred tomb where it is my fate to be buried in this land .
This way , here — come this way ! Hermes the Conductor and the goddess of the dead lead me in this direction .
Light of day , no light to me , once you were mine ,

sections.label: Section 1550
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἔσχατόν σου τοὐμὸν ἅπτεται δέμας .
sections.translation: but now my body feels you for the last time ! For now I go to hide the end of my life in the house of Hades . But you , dearest of strangers , may you yourself be prosperous , and this land , and your followers . In your prosperity ,

sections.label: Section 1555
sections.original: μέμνησθέ μου θανόντος εὐτυχεῖς ἀεί .
sections.translation: remember me in my death , and be fortunate evermore . He exits , followed by his daughters , Theseus , and attendants .

sections.label: Section 1.1556
sections.original: εἰ θέμις ἐστί μοι τὰν ἀφανῆ θεὸν
sections.translation: If it is right for me with prayer to adore the Unseen Goddess and you , Lord of the Dead , then hear me , Aidoneus , Aidoneus !

sections.label: Section 1.1560
sections.original: Αἰδωνεῦ Αἰδωνεῦ , λίσσωμαι
sections.translation: Grant that without pain , without a fate arousing heavy grief , the stranger may pass to the all-concealing fields of the dead below , and to the Stygian house .

sections.label: Section 1.1565
sections.original: πολλῶν γὰρ ἂν καὶ μάταν
sections.translation: Many were the sorrows that came to him without cause , but a just divinity will lift him up again .

sections.label: Section 1.1568
sections.original: ὦ χθόνιαι θεαὶ σῶμά τʼ ἀνικάτου
sections.translation: Goddesses of the nether world and unconquered beast

sections.label: Section 1.1570
sections.original: ταῖσι πολυξένοις
sections.translation: whose lair lies in the gates of many guests , you untamable Watcher of Hades , snarling from the cavern ’ s jaws , as rumor has always told ! Hear me , Death , son of Earth and Tartarus !

sections.label: Section 1.1575
sections.original: κατεύχομαι ἐν καθαρῷ βῆναι
sections.translation: May that Watcher leave a clear path for the stranger on his way to the nether fields of the dead ! To you I call , giver of the eternal sleep .

sections.label: Section 1579
sections.original: ἄνδρες πολῖται , ξυντομωτάτως , μὲν ἂν
sections.translation: Citizens , my news might be summed up most briefly thus : Oedipus is dead .

sections.label: Section 1580
sections.original: τύχοιμι λέξας Οἰδίπουν ὀλωλότα ·
sections.translation: But the story of the happening cannot be told in brief words , as the deeds done there were not brief .

sections.label: Section 1583
sections.original: ὄλωλε γὰρ δύστηνος ;
sections.translation: Is he gone , the unfortunate man ?
